domain: Andean Inca quipu (khipu) (cord-knot record system; main cord + pendant cords + subsidiary cords; encoding via knot-position decimal place + color category + spacing place-separator + twist direction + fiber type + knot type)
specific_mechanism: Multi-dimensional encoding via position-color-spacing-twist-knot-fiber channels with explicit hierarchical place-value structure; subsidiary cords carry exceptions/corrections to parent pendant.
llm_application: QUIPU-MEMORY: an external memory architecture where each memory entry e_i carries a tuple of orthogonal channels (pos_code ∈ base-K, color_embed ∈ R^d_c, spacing_sep ∈ {0,1}, twist_flag ∈ {±1}, fiber_type ∈ {0,1,…,F-1}); the entry is indexed by ALL channels jointly. Retrieval is COMBINATORIAL-CHANNEL: query specifies a partial-channel mask (e.g., 'all category=red regardless of pos_code') and the system returns matching entries. Hierarchy is realised via subsidiary entries pointing to a parent_entry_id (exception/correction). Differs from H-MEM (single positional index per layer) by multi-dimensional orthogonal channels + partial-channel mask retrieval.
novelty_claim: H-MEM uses 4-layer hierarchy (Domain/Category/Trace/Episode) with single positional index per memory vector. Mem0/Memory-R1 use extraction-consolidation-forgetting. QUIPU-MEMORY differs by (a) MULTI-DIMENSIONAL ORTHOGONAL CHANNELS per entry (5+ channels: position, category, spacing, twist, fiber, knot-type), (b) PARTIAL-CHANNEL MASK retrieval (any subset of channels can be unspecified), (c) SUBSIDIARY-EXCEPTION mechanism (exceptions/corrections as branched entries), (d) BASE-K positional code (not flat scalar).
content_words: base-K positional code + color category + twist flag

llm_side: base-K positional code + color category + twist flag
